Design a comprehensive plan for redesigning the Notes sidebar in Neuman Brain.

## Current State Issues (from exploration):
1. **Visual**: Dated look, poor spacing, cramped
2. **Layout**: Fixed 40% folders / 60% notes split - inflexible
3. **Interactions**: 1px resize handle, awkward collapse button positioning
4. **Responsive**: No mobile/tablet breakpoints

## Current Implementation:
- File: `src/pages/Notes.tsx` (1300+ lines)
- FolderSidebar section with `max-h-[40%]` constraint
- NotesList section with `flex-1`
- Resizable width (200-500px, default 320px)
- Components: FolderTreeNode (recursive), NoteListItem, TagFilter

## Design Inspirations:
- **Obsidian**: Dense, functional, keyboard-driven, file explorer feel
- **Notion**: Clean, spacious, modern icons, good use of whitespace
- **Linear**: Ultra-clean, opinionated, speed-focused, minimal
- **Apple Notes**: Simple, native feel, low cognitive overhead

## Requirements:
1. Modern visual design with proper spacing and typography
2. Flexible layout - resizable splitter between folders/notes sections
3. Better interactions - larger drag handles, smooth animations
4. Responsive design - collapse on mobile, adapt to screen sizes
5. Maintain all existing features (folders, tags, search, bulk ops)

Create a detailed implementation plan covering:
1. Visual design system changes (colors, spacing, typography)
2. Sidebar component architecture (new vs refactored components)
3. Resizable splitter implementation approach
4. Responsive breakpoints and behavior
5. Animation and transition improvements
6. File structure and component organization
7. Implementation phases with priority order

# src/components/CLAUDE.md — Shared Components ## Scope Reusable React components under `src/components/`. These are shared across pages and widgets. ## Component Categories | Category | Purpose | Examples | |----------|---------|----------| | **Layout** | Page structure | `Layout.tsx`, `Sidebar.tsx`, `Header.tsx`, `Footer.tsx`, `PageHeader.tsx`, `PageContent.tsx` | | **Modals** | Dialog overlays | `Modal.tsx`, `ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `PromptDialog.tsx`, `*Modal.tsx` | | **Calendar/Time** | Date/time UI | `DayView.tsx`, `WeekView.tsx`, `AgendaView.tsx`, `TimeEntry*.tsx` | | **Widget System** | Widget infrastructure | `Widget.tsx`, `WidgetControls.tsx`, `WidgetManager.tsx`, `WidgetErrorBoundary.tsx` | | **Analytics** | Charts & metrics | `Analytics/*` (MetricCard, CompletionRateChart, PriorityDistribution, StatusBreakdown, TimeByProjectChart, HourlyHeatmap, SessionDurationChart, MeetingVsFocusChart, TagFrequencyChart, AnalyticsPeriodSelector) | | **Graph** | Knowledge graph visualization | `Graph/GraphCanvas.tsx` (D3 force-directed graph with zoom/pan/drag) | | **Diagrams** | Diagram editor | `DiagramCanvas/Canvas.tsx` (Konva Stage with shapes, grid, transformer), `DiagramCanvas/Toolbar.tsx` (tool selection) | | **Settings** | Configuration UI | `BackupSettings.tsx`, `ProviderSettings.tsx`, `ModelSelector.tsx`, `PresetManager.tsx` | | **Error Handling** | Error UI | `StoreErrorBoundary.tsx`, `ErrorToast.tsx`, `Toast.tsx` | | **Terminal** | AI/Shell UI | `AITerminal.tsx`, `TerminalHelpModal.tsx`, `PhantomShell/*` | | **Editors** | Rich text/specialized | `editor/*`, `RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `BackgroundCustomizer.tsx` | | **Tags** | Tag management UI | `TagPicker.tsx`, `TagFilter.tsx`, `TagManager.tsx` | | **Projects** | Project context UI | `ProjectTree.tsx`, `ProjectContextDropdown.tsx`, `ProjectPicker.tsx`, `ProjectSettingsModal.tsx` | ## Non-negotiables - **Props typing:** All props must have TypeScript interfaces. No `any`. - **Semantic tokens:** Use Tailwind semantic classes (`bg-surface-*`, `text-text-*`). No hardcoded colors. - **Accessibility:** ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus management. - **Error boundaries:** Wrap async/complex components in error boundaries. - **Keep focused:** One component = one responsibility. Split if >300 lines. ## Component Pattern ```tsx interface MyComponentProps { title: string; onAction: () => void; variant?: 'primary' | 'secondary'; } export function MyComponent({ title, onAction, variant = 'primary' }: MyComponentProps) { return (

{title}

); } ``` ## Subfolders - `__tests__/` — Component tests (Vitest + Testing Library) - `Analytics/` — Analytics charts and metric components (Recharts-based) - `auth/` — Authentication-related components - `CommandPalette/` — Global search & command palette (Ctrl+K/Cmd+K) - `DiagramCanvas/` — Diagram editor components (Konva-based) - `editor/` — Rich text editor components (Lexical) - `PhantomShell/` — Terminal emulation components - `search/` — Search-related components - `shared/` — Common primitives - `sidebar/` — Sidebar-specific components ## Component Development **For component API design** (choosing props, simplification): - Use **`/consider:via-negativa`** to identify what props/features to remove - Use **`/consider:occams-razor`** when choosing between complex patterns - Example: "Should we support both controlled and uncontrolled modes, or just controlled?" **For creating project-specific component scaffolding**: - Use **`/create-slash-command`** to build component scaffolding command - Example: Create `/scaffold-component` for consistent component boilerplate See [Tache Integration Guide § Component Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-component-development) for complete workflow. --- ## Patterns & Solutions ### Drag-and-Drop with Navigation Links (@dnd-kit) **Problem:** When using @dnd-kit with React Router Links, native browser click events can fire during/after drag operations, causing unwanted navigation. **Solution Pattern (from Sidebar.tsx):** ```tsx const [isDisablingClicks, setIsDisablingClicks] = useState(false); const disableTimeoutRef = useRef(null); const handleDragStart = () => { // Disable clicks IMMEDIATELY when drag starts if (disableTimeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(disableTimeoutRef.current); setIsDisablingClicks(true); }; const handleDragEnd = (event: DragEndEvent) => { // Keep clicks disabled for 150ms after drag ends disableTimeoutRef.current = setTimeout(() => { setIsDisablingClicks(false); disableTimeoutRef.current = null; }, 150); }; // Apply pointer-events control to list items
  • { if (isDisablingClicks) { e.preventDefault(); return false; } }}>
    • {/* sortable items */} ``` **Why both approaches:** - `pointer-events: none` blocks native browser clicks (most important) - `onClick preventDefault` provides React-level safety (defense in depth) - Timeout in ref survives component re-renders from state updates **Key insights:** - Disable clicks on `onDragStart`, not just `onDragEnd` - Mouse release can occur over Link text, not just drag handle - @dnd-kit can fire native clicks BEFORE React handlers execute - Store timeout ID in ref to survive re-renders --- ### PageContent Variants and Scrolling **Problem:** When fixing virtualized scrolling (react-window) for pages like Link Library, setting `overflow-hidden` on Layout.tsx breaks scrolling on pages that need traditional document scrolling (Settings, Dashboard, etc.). **Root Cause (commit 752a7d9):** - Layout.tsx main element had `overflow-hidden` to prevent outer page scrollbar - This blocked ALL scrolling, including for pages with long content - Pages using `variant="default"` had no scroll capability **Solution Pattern (PageContent.tsx):** ```tsx const variantClasses: Record = { // Standard page - scrolls within flex container (Settings, Dashboard, etc.) default: 'flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0', // Full-height - fills viewport, internal scrolling (Graph, Notes, Kanban) 'full-height': 'flex flex-col flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-hidden', // Split-view - horizontal layout for sidebar + content (Link Library) 'split-view': 'flex flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-hidden', }; ``` **Key principles:** 1. **`default` variant:** Enables `overflow-y-auto` for traditional page scrolling 2. **`full-height`/`split-view`:** Use `overflow-hidden` so children handle their own scrolling (virtualized lists, canvas, etc.) 3. **`min-h-0`:** Required on all variants for flexbox children to shrink and scroll properly **When to use each variant:** | Variant | Use Case | Examples | |---------|----------|----------| | `default` | Long-form content, forms | Settings, Dashboard, Habits | | `full-height` | Canvas, virtualized content | Graph, Diagrams | | `split-view` | Sidebar + scrollable main | Link Library, Notes | **Anti-pattern:** - Setting `overflow-hidden` at layout level without providing scroll capability in PageContent variants - Forgetting `min-h-0` on flex containers (children won't scroll) --- ### Hover Actions without Overlap **Problem:** Absolute positioned hover actions (edit, delete, etc.) can overlap with content titles/text, making both unreadable. Common in card/list item components. **Solution Pattern (from Notes.tsx):** ```tsx // ❌ BEFORE: Absolute positioning causes overlap
    {/* Static indicators compete with hover actions */}
    {note.isFavorite && } {note.isPinned && }
    {/* Title can overflow into action space */}

    {note.title}

    {/* Actions overlap title when revealed */}
    {/* 5 buttons - can overlap long titles */}
    // ✅ AFTER: Flex layout with reserved action zone
    {/* Header row: Title + Actions */}
    {/* Title: grows to fill space, truncates before actions */}

    {note.title}

    {/* Reserved action zone: prevents overlap */}
    {/* Static indicators: always visible */}
    {note.isFavorite && } {note.isPinned && }
    {/* Hover actions: occupy space even when invisible */}
    {/* Content below */}

    {content}

    ``` **For images/media with controls (from ImageNode.tsx):** ```tsx // ❌ BEFORE: Negative margin can overlap content below
    {/* Controls overlap content below image */}
    // ✅ AFTER: Controls positioned with positive margin
    ``` **Key principles:** 1. **Flex over Absolute:** Use flex layout for title + actions in same row 2. **Reserve Space:** Actions use `opacity-0` (not `hidden`) to preserve layout 3. **Title Flexibility:** `flex-1 min-w-0` allows title to shrink/truncate gracefully 4. **Action Zone:** `shrink-0` prevents action container from collapsing 5. **Positive Spacing:** Use `mt-*` instead of negative margins for media controls **Advantages:** - ✅ Zero overlap at any viewport width or content length - ✅ No layout shift when actions appear/disappear - ✅ Predictable behavior with fixed action widths - ✅ Responsive title truncation **When to use:** - Card components with hover actions (Notes, Tasks, Forms, etc.) - List items with edit/delete/duplicate buttons - Media elements (images, videos) with control overlays --- ### Avoiding Tab/Button Redundancy **Problem:** Creating tabs that filter the same content (e.g., "Documents", "Spreadsheets", "Presentations" tabs that all show docs filtered by type) when direct action buttons would be simpler and more discoverable. **Bad Pattern (from Docs.tsx - fixed 2025-12-11):** ```tsx // ❌ BEFORE: 4 tabs that show the same content with filters const CREATE_TABS: Tab[] = [ { id: 'create', label: 'Create', icon: Sparkles }, { id: 'documents', label: 'Documents', icon: FileText }, // Redundant { id: 'spreadsheets', label: 'Spreadsheets', icon: Table2 }, // Redundant { id: 'presentations', label: 'Presentations', icon: Presentation }, // Redundant ]; // + A dropdown button in sidebar: {showMenu && } // Dropdown with Document/Sheet/Slides // + Another dropdown in empty state center (same options) ``` **Good Pattern:** ```tsx // ✅ AFTER: Only meaningful tabs (distinct content types) const CREATE_TABS: Tab[] = [ { id: 'create', label: 'Create', icon: Sparkles }, // All docs { id: 'diagrams', label: 'Diagrams', icon: Shapes }, // Different content { id: 'forms', label: 'Forms', icon: ClipboardList }, // Different content ]; // Direct action buttons (no dropdown needed):
    // Empty state: Just informational (actions in sidebar)

    Documents, Spreadsheets & Presentations

    Create professional documents...

    {/* No duplicate create buttons */}
    ``` **Key principles:** 1. **Tabs = Distinct Content:** Tabs should show meaningfully different content, not filtered versions of the same list 2. **Direct Actions > Dropdowns:** If you have 2-4 primary actions and space allows, use direct buttons instead of a dropdown menu 3. **Single Point of Action:** Don't duplicate the same action in multiple places (sidebar + center empty state) 4. **Discoverability:** Direct buttons are more discoverable than dropdown menus **Questions to ask:** - "Do these tabs show genuinely different content, or just filtered views?" - "Is the dropdown hiding actions that could be direct buttons?" - "Am I duplicating the same action in multiple places?" --- ## Anti-patterns - Hardcoded colors/sizes (use semantic tokens). - Business logic in components (move to stores/services). - Props drilling >2 levels (use stores or context). - Missing TypeScript types. - No accessibility attributes on interactive elements. - Over-engineering component APIs (use **`/consider:via-negativa`** to simplify). - Using @dnd-kit with Links without click protection (see Patterns section above). - Absolute positioning for hover actions near text content (causes overlap - see Hover Actions pattern). - Negative margins for hover controls (can overlap adjacent content - use positive spacing instead). - Using `hidden` class for hover actions (causes layout shift - use `opacity-0` to preserve space). - Creating tabs that filter the same content instead of using direct action buttons (see Tab/Button Redundancy pattern). - Duplicating the same action in multiple UI locations (e.g., sidebar + empty state center). ## Related - Widgets (consumers): `src/widgets/CLAUDE.md` - Stores (state): `src/stores/CLAUDE.md` - Tests: `tests/CLAUDE.md`

Now I have gathered enough information. Let me provide the comprehensive implementation plan based on my exploration: --- # Notes Sidebar Redesign - Comprehensive Implementation Plan ## Executive Summary This plan outlines a comprehensive redesign of the Notes sidebar in Neuman Brain, addressing the identified issues of dated visuals, inflexible layout, poor interactions, and lack of responsive design. The redesign will follow the existing design system while introducing modern patterns inspired by Obsidian, Notion, Linear, and Apple Notes. --- ## Part 1: Current State Analysis ### Existing Implementation (src/pages/Notes.tsx - 1377 lines) **Current Structure:** - `FolderSidebar` component (lines 225-502): Fixed `max-h-[40%]` constraint - `NotesList` component (lines 733-1015): Uses `flex-1` for remaining space - Sidebar width: 200-500px range, default 320px - Resize handle: 1px wide (`w-1`), positioned at right edge - Collapse button: Absolute positioned `absolute top-4 right-2` **Current Issues Identified:** 1. **Visual**: Inconsistent spacing (p-4 vs px-3 py-2), cramped folder items 2. **Layout**: Fixed 40/60 split with `max-h-[40%]` - not resizable between sections 3. **Interactions**: 1px resize handle is difficult to grab, no visual feedback on hover 4. **Responsive**: No breakpoints, sidebar just collapses entirely --- ## Part 2: Design System Alignment ### Existing Tokens (from tailwind.config.js and index.css) **Spacing:** - `widget-padding`: 16px - `content-gap`: 16px - `button-gap`: 8px - `grid-gap`: 24px **Colors (semantic):** - Surface: `--surface-light`, `--surface-light-elevated`, `--surface-dark`, `--surface-dark-elevated` - Accent: `--accent-cyan`, `--accent-magenta`, `--accent-blue` - Border: `--border-light`, `--border-dark` **Border Radius:** - `rounded-card`: 12px - `rounded-button`: 8px - `rounded-input`: 6px **Transitions:** - `duration-fast`: 100ms - `duration-standard`: 200ms - `duration-slow`: 300ms - `ease-smooth`: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) --- ## Part 3: Proposed Architecture ### Component Structure ``` src/ ├── components/ │ └── NotesSidebar/ │ ├── index.ts # Barrel export │ ├── NotesSidebar.tsx # Main container │ ├── FolderPanel.tsx # Folder tree section │ ├── NotesPanel.tsx # Notes list section │ ├── ResizableSplitter.tsx # Reusable vertical splitter │ ├── FolderTreeNode.tsx # Extracted from Notes.tsx │ ├── NoteListItem.tsx # Extracted from Notes.tsx │ ├── SidebarHeader.tsx # Search + collapse controls │ ├── SidebarFooter.tsx # Tag filter + actions │ └── __tests__/ │ ├── NotesSidebar.test.tsx │ ├── ResizableSplitter.test.tsx │ └── FolderTreeNode.test.tsx └── hooks/ └── useResizableSplitter.ts # Splitter logic hook ``` ### New Component: ResizableSplitter ```typescript interface ResizableSplitterProps { /** Current position (0-100 as percentage or pixels) */ position: number; /** Callback when position changes */ onPositionChange: (position: number) => void; /** Minimum position */ min?: number; /** Maximum position */ max?: number; /** Direction of split */ direction?: 'horizontal' | 'vertical'; /** Size mode */ mode?: 'percentage' | 'pixels'; } ``` **Features:** - 8px drag handle (4px actual + 4px hover expansion) - Visual feedback: subtle line becomes accent color on hover - Cursor changes: `cursor-row-resize` (horizontal) or `cursor-col-resize` (vertical) - Keyboard accessible: Arrow keys to adjust, Enter to confirm - Double-click to reset to default (50%) --- ## Part 4: Visual Design Specifications ### Spacing Improvements | Element | Current | Proposed | |---------|---------|----------| | Folder item height | h-8 (32px) | h-10 (40px) | | Folder item padding | px-3 py-2 | px-4 py-2.5 | | Section header | mb-4 | mb-6 | | Between items | space-y-1 | space-y-2 | | Sidebar padding | p-4 | p-5 | | Note card padding | p-4 | p-5 | ### Typography Adjustments | Element | Current | Proposed | |---------|---------|----------| | Section headers | text-base font-semibold | text-sm font-medium uppercase tracking-wide | | Folder names | text-base | text-sm font-medium | | Note titles | font-medium | text-sm font-semibold | | Note preview | text-sm line-clamp-2 | text-xs line-clamp-2 text-secondary | | Note date | text-xs | text-[11px] text-tertiary | ### Visual Enhancements **Folder Items:** ```css /* Current */ rounded-lg transition-all duration-standard /* Proposed */ rounded-lg transition-all duration-fast hover:bg-surface-light-elevated/50 dark:hover:bg-surface-dark-elevated/50 active:scale-[0.98] ``` **Active States:** ```css /* Current */ bg-accent-cyan bg-opacity-10 dark:bg-accent-magenta dark:bg-opacity-10 /* Proposed - Left accent bar (Linear-inspired) */ bg-transparent border-l-2 border-accent-cyan dark:border-accent-magenta pl-[14px] /* compensate for border */ ``` **Note Cards:** ```css /* Current */ border-l-4 border-accent-cyan dark:border-accent-magenta shadow-sm /* Proposed - Cleaner selection */ bg-surface-light-elevated dark:bg-surface-dark-elevated ring-1 ring-accent-cyan/30 dark:ring-accent-magenta/30 ``` --- ## Part 5: Resizable Splitter Implementation ### Hook: useResizableSplitter ```typescript // src/hooks/useResizableSplitter.ts interface UseResizableSplitterOptions { initialPosition: number; min: number; max: number; direction: 'horizontal' | 'vertical'; onPositionChange?: (position: number) => void; persistKey?: string; // For localStorage persistence } interface UseResizableSplitterReturn { position: number; isDragging: boolean; handleMouseDown: (e: React.MouseEvent) => void; handleKeyDown: (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => void; resetToDefault: () => void; } ``` **Implementation Details:** 1. Use `useEffect` for global mousemove/mouseup listeners during drag 2. Store position in localStorage if `persistKey` provided 3. Debounce position changes for performance 4. Support touch events for mobile ### Splitter Visual Design ```tsx // Expanded drag handle area with subtle visual indicator
{/* Visual line - centered within handle area */}
{/* Grip dots (on hover) */}

``` --- ## Part 6: Responsive Design ### Breakpoints Strategy | Breakpoint | Width | Sidebar Behavior | |------------|-------|------------------| | Mobile | <640px | Full-screen overlay, swipe to dismiss | | Tablet | 640-1024px | Collapsible, narrower (240px) | | Desktop | 1024px+ | Full sidebar (320px default) | ### Mobile Implementation ```tsx // Mobile: Sidebar as drawer overlay {isMobile && ( )} // Mobile: Backdrop {isMobile && isSidebarOpen && ( setIsSidebarOpen(false)} className="fixed inset-0 bg-black z-40" /> )} ``` ### Tablet Adaptations ```tsx // Tablet: Narrower sidebar, single section visible at a time const [activeSection, setActiveSection] = useState<'folders' | 'notes'>('folders'); // Toggle between sections with tabs
``` --- ## Part 7: Animation Improvements ### Transitions to Add **Folder Expand/Collapse:** ```tsx {children} ``` **Sidebar Width Change:** ```tsx ``` **Note Selection:** ```tsx // Subtle scale + background transition ``` **Splitter Drag Feedback:** ```tsx // During drag, add subtle shadow/glow
``` --- ## Part 8: Implementation Phases ### Phase 1: Extract and Reorganize (Priority: High) **Estimated time:** 2-3 hours **Tasks:** 1. Create `src/components/NotesSidebar/` folder structure 2. Extract `FolderTreeNode` component from Notes.tsx 3. Extract `NoteListItem` component from Notes.tsx 4. Create barrel exports and update imports in Notes.tsx 5. Verify functionality unchanged **Files to modify:** - Create: `src/components/NotesSidebar/FolderTreeNode.tsx` - Create: `src/components/NotesSidebar/NoteListItem.tsx` - Create: `src/components/NotesSidebar/index.ts` - Modify: `src/pages/Notes.tsx` (import updates) ### Phase 2: Resizable Splitter (Priority: High) **Estimated time:** 3-4 hours **Tasks:** 1. Create `useResizableSplitter` hook 2. Create `ResizableSplitter` component 3. Replace fixed 40/60 split with resizable splitter 4. Persist splitter position in localStorage 5. Add keyboard accessibility (arrow keys) **Files to create/modify:** - Create: `src/hooks/useResizableSplitter.ts` - Create: `src/components/NotesSidebar/ResizableSplitter.tsx` - Modify: `src/components/NotesSidebar/NotesSidebar.tsx` ### Phase 3: Visual Refresh (Priority: High) **Estimated time:** 2-3 hours **Tasks:** 1. Update spacing according to design spec 2. Implement new active state styling (left accent bar) 3. Improve resize handle visibility (8px area) 4. Update collapse button positioning 5. Refine typography hierarchy **Files to modify:** - `src/components/NotesSidebar/FolderTreeNode.tsx` - `src/components/NotesSidebar/NoteListItem.tsx` - `src/components/NotesSidebar/NotesSidebar.tsx` ### Phase 4: Animations (Priority: Medium) **Estimated time:** 2 hours **Tasks:** 1. Add smooth folder expand/collapse with Framer Motion 2. Add sidebar width animation on resize 3. Add subtle hover/tap feedback on notes 4. Add drag feedback on splitter **Files to modify:** - `src/components/NotesSidebar/FolderTreeNode.tsx` - `src/components/NotesSidebar/NotesSidebar.tsx` - `src/components/NotesSidebar/ResizableSplitter.tsx` ### Phase 5: Responsive Design (Priority: Medium) **Estimated time:** 3-4 hours **Tasks:** 1. Implement mobile drawer overlay 2. Add tablet tab-based section switching 3. Update collapse/expand behavior per breakpoint 4. Add touch gestures (swipe to close on mobile) 5. Test across breakpoints **Files to create/modify:** - Create: `src/hooks/useBreakpoint.ts` (if not exists) - Modify: `src/components/NotesSidebar/NotesSidebar.tsx` - Modify: `src/pages/Notes.tsx` ### Phase 6: Testing and Polish (Priority: High) **Estimated time:** 2-3 hours **Tasks:** 1. Add unit tests for new hooks 2. Add component tests for splitter 3. Manual testing of all interactions 4. Performance profiling (avoid re-renders during drag) 5. Accessibility audit (keyboard nav, screen readers) **Files to create:** - `src/hooks/__tests__/useResizableSplitter.test.ts` - `src/components/NotesSidebar/__tests__/ResizableSplitter.test.tsx` - `src/components/NotesSidebar/__tests__/FolderTreeNode.test.tsx` --- ## Part 9: Keyboard Shortcuts ### Existing (to preserve) - `Cmd+K`: Focus search - `Cmd+N`: New note ### New (to add) - `Cmd+\`: Toggle sidebar - `Cmd+Shift+E`: Focus notes list - `Cmd+Shift+F`: Focus folder tree - `Arrow Up/Down`: Navigate items - `Enter`: Select item - `Escape`: Clear selection / close mobile sidebar --- ## Part 10: Accessibility Considerations 1. **ARIA attributes** on ResizableSplitter (role="separator", aria-valuenow, etc.) 2. **Focus management** when sidebar toggles 3. **Skip links** to jump between sections 4. **Screen reader announcements** for drag operations 5. **Reduced motion** support via `prefers-reduced-motion` --- ## Part 11: Performance Considerations 1. **Memoization**: Memoize `FolderTreeNode` and `NoteListItem` components 2. **Virtualization**: Consider `react-window` if note list exceeds 100 items 3. **Debounce**: Debounce resize position updates (16ms RAF) 4. **CSS transforms**: Use `transform` for animations (GPU-accelerated) 5. **Will-change**: Add `will-change: transform` during drag operations --- ## Critical Files for Implementation | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\pages\Notes.tsx` | Main page to refactor -
